📄 Data Format Guide

Find LESSONS_DATA in the HTML source to add your real questions:

// MCQ (optionReasons array explains each choice)
{ id:"L1_MCQ_001", type:"mcq", text:"...", options:["A","B","C","D"], answer:0,
  optionReasons:["Correct: because...","Wrong: because...","Wrong: because...","Wrong: because..."],
  explanation:"Overall note." }

// True/False
{ id:"L1_TF_001", type:"truefalse", text:"...", answer:true, explanation:"..." }

// Matching (dropdown select)
{ id:"L1_M_001", type:"matching", text:"...", pairs:[{term:"...",definition:"..."}], explanation:"..." }

// Short Answer (reveal + self-assess — no typing needed)
{ id:"L1_SA_001", type:"shortanswer", text:"...", answer:"...", explanation:"..." }

// Compare (reveal table + self-assess)
{ id:"L1_C_001", type:"compare", text:"...", aspects:["Aspect"],
  itemA:{name:"A",values:["val"]}, itemB:{name:"B",values:["val"]}, explanation:"..." }

// Add image to ANY type: { ..., image:"data:image/png;base64,...", ... }

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// TRUE/FALSE — Single statement (classic)
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
{ id:"L1_TF_001", type:"truefalse",
  text:"Glucose is the main blood sugar.",
  answer:true,
  explanation:"Glucose is the primary circulating sugar in the blood." }

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// TRUE/FALSE GROUP — Multiple statements, one T or F each
// Exactly like exam questions: "Indicate T or F for each:"
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
{ id:"L1_MSTF_001", type:"multitf",
  text:"For sugars — indicate True (T) or False (F) for each statement:",
  statements:[
    { text:"Sucrose is a non reducing sugar",           answer:true  },
    { text:"Glucose and mannose are epimers",           answer:true  },
    { text:"Fructose is the main blood sugar",          answer:false },
    { text:"Amino sugars are constituents of proteoglycans", answer:true },
    { text:"Heparin acts as anticoagulant",             answer:true  }
  ],
  explanation:"Sucrose has no free anomeric OH → non-reducing. Mannose differs from glucose at C-2 (epimers). Glucose, not fructose, is the main blood sugar." }
